title: Palak Paneer (Spinach with Cottage Cheese)

ingredients.title: For the Paneer
items: 250 g paneer (Indian cottage cheese), cut into cubes
1 tsp ghee or oil (optional, for lightly sautéing)

ingredients.title: For the Spinach Gravy
items: 500 g fresh spinach leaves, washed and chopped

ingredients.title: Finishing Touch
items: 2 tbsp fresh cream or yogurt (optional for richness)

instruction.title: Method
steps: Blanch the Spinach: Boil water in a large pan. Add a pinch of salt.
Add spinach leaves and blanch for 2 minutes.
Drain and immediately transfer to ice-cold water to retain the green color.
Blend spinach into a smooth puree using a little water.
Prepare the Masala: Heat 1 tbsp oil in a pan. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter.
Add onions, sauté until golden brown.
Add ginger, garlic, and green chilies; sauté for 1–2 minutes.
Add tomatoes, turmeric, red chili powder, and salt. Cook until tomatoes are soft and oil starts separating.
Let the mixture cool slightly, then blend into a smooth paste (optional for a silky texture).
Cook the Palak Gravy: In a pan, combine the spinach puree and the onion-tomato paste.
Simmer for 5–7 minutes on low heat.
Add garam masala and adjust salt.
Add Paneer: Lightly sauté paneer cubes in ghee or oil for a minute to keep them soft (optional).
Add paneer cubes to the spinach gravy. Cook for 2–3 minutes.
Stir in cream or yogurt for richness.
Finish: Check seasoning and add a few drops of lemon juice to balance flavors.
Garnish with a small dollop of butter or cream.
Serve hot with roti, naan, or jeera rice.

chefTips: Serve hot with roti, naan, or jeera rice.
